name: Azure Storage Credential Reconnaissance via PowerShell
description: Detects PowerShell accessing Azure CLI credential files (accessTokens.json, azureProfile.json, service_principal_entries.json) or Azure Storage Explorer configuration directories. This behavior is consistent with cloud credential harvesting prior to data exfiltration operations. Real attackers targeting cloud-hosted data routinely enumerate Azure CLI profiles and cached tokens to obtain storage account keys or OAuth tokens before exfiltrating data to cloud storage services.
query:
file where host.os.type == "windows" and
  process.name in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and
  (
    file.path : ("*\\StorageExplorer\\*", "*\\Azure Storage Explorer\\*", "*\\.azure\\*", "*\\AzureStorageExplorer\\*") or
    file.name in~ ("accessTokens.json", "azureProfile.json", "service_principal_entries.json", "msal_http_cache", "msal_token_cache.json")
  )
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Recursive File Enumeration Targeting High-Value Data Types
description: Detects PowerShell performing recursive directory enumeration across user-profile directories (Documents, Desktop, Downloads, AppData, ProgramData) with filters targeting sensitive file extensions (*.pdf, *.docx, *.xlsx, *.pfx, *.pem, *.sql). This pattern is characteristic of automated data staging reconnaissance performed before exfiltration. The Get-ChildItem -Recurse pattern combined with high-value extension filters across multiple user directories is a reliable behavioral indicator of pre-exfiltration data discovery.
query:
process where host.os.type == "windows" and
  process.name in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and
  process.command_line : "*Get-ChildItem*" and
  process.command_line : "*-Recurse*" and
  (
    process.command_line : ("*Documents*", "*Desktop*", "*Downloads*", "*AppData*", "*ProgramData*") or
    process.command_line : ("*.pdf*", "*.docx*", "*.xlsx*", "*.p12*", "*.pfx*", "*.pem*", "*.sql*", "*.accdb*")
  )
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Process Masquerading - System Binary Executing from Non-Standard Path
description: Detects critical Windows system binaries (conhost.exe, svchost.exe, lsass.exe, csrss.exe) executing from paths outside their expected System32 or SysWOW64 directories. Process masquerading (T1036.005) is a reliable defense evasion technique used by ransomware and APT actors to blend malicious executables with legitimate Windows processes. conhost.exe executing outside C:\Windows\System32 is a critical high-fidelity indicator — the legitimate console host process is never relocated by the OS.
query:
process where host.os.type == "windows" and
  process.name in~ ("conhost.exe", "svchost.exe", "lsass.exe", "csrss.exe", "wininit.exe", "services.exe") and
  not process.executable : ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*", "C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransom Note Creation Followed by Encrypted File Writes
description: Detects a sequence where a ransom note file (matching common ransom note naming patterns) is created, followed by file writes with ransomware-associated extensions (.encrypted, .enc, .locked) from the same host within 10 minutes. This sequenced EQL detection provides high-confidence ransomware activity correlation (T1486) by requiring both behavioral markers — the ransom note and the encrypted output files — rather than alerting on either indicator in isolation.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=10m
  [file where host.os.type == "windows" and
   event.action == "creation" and
   file.name : ("*DECRYPT*", "*RANSOM*", "*HOW_TO*", "*RESTORE_FILES*", "*RECOVER*", "*README_DECRYPT*", "*LOCKED*", "*_readme*", "*INSTRUCTIONS*") and
   file.extension in~ ("txt", "html", "hta")]
  [file where host.os.type == "windows" and
   event.action == "creation" and
   file.extension in~ ("encrypted", "enc", "locked", "crypt", "crypted")]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Multi-Phase Exfiltration Kill Chain - Recon to Data Staging to Masquerading
description: Detects the full multi-phase exfiltration kill chain: (1) PowerShell performing Azure storage or file system reconnaissance, followed by (2) PowerShell creating a compressed archive in a user-writable directory, followed by (3) execution of a process masquerading as a system binary from a non-standard path. This correlated sequence with a 30-minute window represents a high-confidence detection of coordinated pre-ransomware data exfiltration operations (T1083, T1560.001, T1036.005) as observed in Conti, LockBit, and Cl0p campaigns.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=30m
  [process where host.os.type == "windows" and
   process.name in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and
   (
     process.command_line : ("*StorageExplorer*", "*.azure*", "*accessTokens*", "*azureProfile*") or
     (process.command_line : "*Get-ChildItem*" and process.command_line : "*-Recurse*")
   )]
  [process where host.os.type == "windows" and
   process.name in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and
   process.command_line : "*Compress-Archive*"]
  [process where host.os.type == "windows" and
   process.name in~ ("conhost.exe", "svchost.exe") and
   not process.executable : ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*")]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
